# Session distill — 0cb4907e-adf3-41d2-92f3-6e88dc76565b

**Date**: 2026-05-01
**Conversation focus**: Design the laboratory archive engine (resolving `c-momaa5o9` under the laboratory apparatus build umbrella `c-mom18vmh`).
**Generated by**: distill-subagent (fresh context); inputs were the JSONL transcript and the click subtree under `c-momaa5o9`.

---

## Intent — what Sean asked for

Sean opened the session pointing at `.scratch/handoff-laboratory-archive-design.md` and said "let's work on" it (msg 1). The implicit ask: drive `c-momaa5o9` (archive engine design) to a concluded state, with the spec written into `packages/laboratory/README.md` so the implementation work can pick up.

Sean's two substantive interventions during the session:
- **Reframe to DB-authoritative** (msg 25): "I find references between DB and filesystems to be problematic… make the DB authoritative… provide an extract tool which could be used to materialize a trial on disk." This reversed Coco's initial Hybrid recommendation and reshaped the whole design.
- **Schema simplification pokes** (msg 37): scenarioWritId not abstract enough; codex remote URL belongs on the trial writ, not duplicated; metadata bag namespace unclear; books-archive and git-diff-archive too specific — should be probe contributions.

End-of-session ask (msg 80): update the implementation handoff doc to reflect the design.

---

## Questions raised this session

- ✓ (resolved) Hybrid (C) vs DB-authoritative (A) vs sanctum-mirror (B)? → DB-authoritative with extract tool. **D1**.
- ✓ (resolved) Per-row indexed columns vs pure JSON1? → Pure JSON1. (Sean, msg 27.)
- ✓ (resolved) Big-diff cap — 5MB or 10MB? → 10MB to start. (Sean, msg 27.)
- ✓ (resolved) Annotations book or sanctum markdown? → Sanctum markdown referencing trialId. (Sean, msg 27.)
- ✓ (resolved) Probe yields contract — full data in-memory or stream? → In-memory. (Sean, msg 27.)
- ✓ (resolved) Can archive records be linked to trial writs via clerk links? → No; clerk links are writ-to-writ only. Drop the link, use FK on `trialId`. **D2**.
- ✓ (resolved) `scenarioWritId` on archive schema? → Drop; assumes scenario shape that doesn't generalize. **D3**.
- ✓ (resolved) Codex remote URL on archive? → No; looked up from trial writ. **D4**.
- ✓ (resolved) Flat `metadata` bag — who writes? → No bag; per-probe summaries keyed by probe id + engineId in `archive.probes[]`. **D5**.
- ✓ (resolved) Books-archive and git-diff-archive owned by archive engine or by probes? → Probes own their own books; archive engine has no schema opinions about probe data. **D6**.
- ✓ (resolved) Plugin packaging — separate per-probe plugins or bundled? → Bundled in `@shardworks/laboratory` for MVP, with per-probe directory seams designed for clean future extraction. **D7**.
- ✓ (resolved) Probe-contributed extraction logic — MVP-required or v2? → MVP-required (Sean overrode Coco's v2 lean; "we will forget the intent by the time we need to add the lookup"). **D8**.
- ✓ (resolved) Trial-context capture — Option X (`lab.probe-trial-context` probe) or Option Y (archive engine captures `context` directly)? → Option X. **D9**.
- ✓ (resolved) Atomicity — single SQLite transaction wraps archive engine? → Per-engine atomicity, no cross-engine transactions; rig structure forbids spanning transactions. **D10** (corrected from msg-26 implication via msg-70 audit.)
- ⊘ (parked / acknowledged limitation) Adding a third probe pre-extraction-contract requires extract-tool code changes — known v1 limitation noted in spec.

---

## Decisions

### D1 — DB-authoritative archive with on-demand filesystem materialization
**Status**: accepted (supersedes earlier in-session recommendation of Hybrid C)
**Context**: Three options analyzed (A: all in lab books; B: sanctum mirror; C: hybrid). Coco initially recommended C; Sean pushed back on DB↔filesystem references being brittle when filesystem is user-owned, proposed pure-DB + extract tool. Pressure-test of the reframe held: query power strictly stronger than B; atomicity simpler (single transaction); extract is sub-second; sanctum stays clean.
**Decision**: Trial data lives in lab guild's stacks DB; on-demand materialization via `nsg lab trial-extract`.
**Consequences**: Browse-without-extract is gone (one extra CLI invocation, sub-second). Diff tooling needs an extract pass for `.patch`/`git apply` workflows. Sanctum reorganizations no longer drag captured data behind them.
**Reverses**: in-session Coco recommendation at msg 24 (Option C).

### D2 — FK linkage only; no clerk link
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: `WritLinkDoc` (sourceId, targetId) requires both endpoints to be writs. Archive records aren't writs. Originally proposed `laboratory.archived-as` link kind (msg 24, msg 26).
**Decision**: Drop the clerk link. Trial→archive relation is `lab-trial-archives.trialId` as FK into `clerk/writs.id`.
**Consequences**: `nsg writ show <trialId>` and `nsg lab trial-show <trialId>` are independent queries. No clerk machinery involved.

### D3 — Drop `scenarioWritId` from archive metadata
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: Field assumed scenarios always produce a writ in test guild — only true for `commission-post-xguild` shape.
**Decision**: Remove the field. Scenario-specific data lives in scenario engine yields, not archive metadata.

### D4 — Codex remote URL not duplicated in archive
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: Manifest carries upstream org/repo/baseSha on the trial writ.
**Decision**: Archive carries only what apparatus learned at runtime (`headSha`, `commitCount`, `totalDiffBytes`).

### D5 — No flat `metadata` bag; per-probe summaries instead
**Status**: accepted (supersedes msg-26 schema sketch)
**Context**: `metadata` bag was vague about ownership and namespace.
**Decision**: Each probe's summary lives in `archive.probes[i].summary`, keyed by probe id + engineId. No shared bag.

### D6 — Books are probe-contributed, not archive-core
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: Sean's poke that books-archive and git-diff-archive are too specific to be archive infrastructure. Archive engine should own only the index.
**Decision**: Archive engine owns one book (`lab-trial-archives`); probes own their own data books and schemas. `lab.probe-stacks-dump` owns `lab-trial-stacks-dumps`; `lab.probe-git-range` owns `lab-trial-codex-commits`.
**Consequences**: Future probes (e.g., network-trace, transcript-capture) contribute their own books without archive-engine changes.

### D7 — Bundled packaging with per-probe directory seams
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: Tradeoff between bundled (one install, MVP speed) and per-probe plugins (books only land if installed; probe substitutability first-class).
**Decision**: Bundle for MVP. Per-probe directory layout (`src/probes/<name>/{engine,book,extractor}.ts`) keeps future extraction mechanical.
**Consequences**: When v2 needs third-party probes, lift `src/probes/<name>/*` into a new package — no architectural surgery.

### D8 — Probe registry + dynamic dispatch is MVP-required (not v2)
**Status**: accepted (Coco recommended v2; Sean overrode)
**Context**: Coco proposed deferring the probe-contributed extract contract to v2 with hardcoded MVP dispatch; Sean: "we will forget the intent by the time we need to add the lookup."
**Decision**: Probe registry + dynamic extract dispatch is MVP-required. New click `c-momkil4p` filed.

### D9 — Trial-context capture via `lab.probe-trial-context` (Option X)
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: Capture rig+template+framework SHA+resolved pins+manifest snapshot. Two options: probe (X) or archive-direct (Y).
**Decision**: Option X — probe in default rig template; summary lands in `archive.probes[]`.
**Consequences**: Archive engine stays at "minimal index of what probes ran." Default trial-template includes the probe; custom templates make a deliberate choice. Folds into existing `c-momaa3w7` (probe engines) implementation work.

### D10 — Per-engine atomicity, not cross-engine transactions
**Status**: accepted (corrected from msg-26 implication after msg-70 audit)
**Context**: msg-26 implied a single SQLite transaction wrapping the archive engine. msg-70 audit caught that rigs run each engine separately — can't span transactions across engines.
**Decision**: Atomicity is per-engine. Archive row is atomic; probe rows are atomic per-probe. Orphans tolerated; queries join from `lab-trial-archives`.
